instructions.instruction: Run full validation using the orchestrator script: `bash scripts/validate_azure_pipelines.sh <pipeline-file>`
Always run all four validation layers in order: YAML lint, syntax validation, best practices, security scan
instructions.relevant_when: When starting a new pipeline validation workflow

instructions.instruction: Fix YAML lint errors before any other layer — Azure Pipelines YAML is whitespace-significant and indentation errors can silently restructure the pipeline
instructions.relevant_when: When yamllint reports warnings such as trailing spaces or wrong indentation

instructions.instruction: Treat MEDIUM and HIGH security findings as merge blockers; do not approve merges until they are resolved
instructions.relevant_when: When security scan reports MEDIUM or HIGH severity findings

instructions.instruction: Run --strict only after fixing all critical errors and warnings on a pipeline; never apply --strict to a brand-new pipeline as the first validation step
instructions.relevant_when: When considering whether to enable strict mode on a pipeline

instructions.instruction: Always run security scan (`--security-only`) before merging pipeline changes — syntax passing does not imply security passing
instructions.relevant_when: When a pipeline change is being prepared for merge

instructions.instruction: Validate every template file referenced in the pipeline, not only the main azure-pipelines.yml entry point
instructions.relevant_when: When a pipeline uses `template:` references to other YAML files

instructions.instruction: Pin all container images to specific versions or SHA digests; never accept `:latest` tags
instructions.relevant_when: When a pipeline defines container resources or uses container images in jobs

instructions.instruction: Report security findings with rule code, severity, line number, and a specific remediation step
instructions.relevant_when: When presenting security scan results to the user

instructions.instruction: When encountering rule codes in brackets (e.g., [missing-displayname]), consult references/azure-pipelines-reference.md for rule details and correct fixes
instructions.relevant_when: When validation output includes bracketed rule codes
